blocks.en: Before Wi-Fi 7, a device picked one band — 2.4, 5, or 6 GHz — and lived there until it roamed. Multi-Link Operation (MLO) lets a device associate over several bands at once and treat them as a single connection.
blocks.zh: 在 Wi-Fi 7 之前，设备会选定一个频段——2.4、5 或 6 GHz——并一直待在那里，直到漫游。多链路操作（MLO）让设备同时在多个频段上关联，并把它们当作一条连接。

blocks.en: Why bother? Three wins: more total throughput (use both bands together), lower latency (send on whichever link is free now), and reliability (if one band gets noisy, the other carries on).
blocks.zh: 为何如此？三大收益：更高的总吞吐（两个频段一起用）、更低的时延（在此刻空闲的链路上发送）、以及可靠性（若一个频段变嘈杂，另一个继续工作）。

blocks.en: But ‘use two links at once’ depends on the hardware. Toggle the links and modes below to see the difference: STR runs links truly in parallel, while single-radio modes (NSTR, EMLSR) can only really use one at a time.
blocks.zh: 但“同时用两条链路”取决于硬件。切换下面的链路与模式看差异：STR 让链路真正并行，而单射频模式（NSTR、EMLSR）实际上一次只能用一条。

blocks.en: STR (Simultaneous Transmit and Receive) is the premium case — two full radios summing their rates. EMLSR (Enhanced Multi-Link Single Radio) is the budget case — one radio that listens on several links and quickly hops to whichever is needed, gaining latency and reliability but not raw sum-throughput.
blocks.zh: STR（同时收发）是高配情形——两套完整射频，速率相加。EMLSR（增强型多链路单射频）是经济情形——一套射频在多条链路上侦听，并迅速切换到所需的那条，换来时延与可靠性，但非原始的吞吐求和。

blocks.en: 802.11be gives MLO its own reference model (4.9.6) and carries the per-link setup in the Multi-Link element (9.4.2.322), whose Basic variant lists each affiliated link. STR/NSTR/EMLSR capabilities are negotiated through these structures.
blocks.zh: 802.11be 为 MLO 设立了专门的参考模型（4.9.6），并在 Multi-Link 元素（9.4.2.322）中承载逐链路的建立，其 Basic 变体列出每条隶属链路。STR/NSTR/EMLSR 能力通过这些结构协商。
